title: Used Bike Online Fraud
description: A seller advertised a used motorcycle at a very attractive price. They asked for ₹15,000 as a booking amount through rajeshstore@okaxis. The seller contacted me using +91 9988776655 and later became unreachable.

title: Mutual Fund Investment Scam
description: A person claiming to work for Prime Wealth Advisory contacted me on +91 9765432109 and offered exclusive mutual fund investments. I transferred ₹84,000 to account 456789XXXX5528 after following instructions from Telegram @PrimeReturnsVIP. I later realized it was fraudulent.

title: Fake IPO Investment Advisory Scam
description: I joined a Telegram group named @PrimeReturnsVIP after receiving a call from +91 9765432109. The advisor claimed to represent Prime Wealth Advisory and promised guaranteed IPO allotments. I transferred ₹75,000 to bank account 456789XXXX5528 but never received any shares or refund.

title: Fake SBI KYC Verification Call
description: I received a call from +91 9876543210 from a person claiming to be an executive of State Bank of India. He said my KYC had expired and my account would be frozen unless I completed verification immediately. I was instructed to transfer ₹48,500 to UPI ID securekyc@ibl. After making the payment, the caller disconnected and became unreachable.

title: Forex Trading Fraud
description: A caller from +91 9765432109 convinced me to invest in forex trading through Prime Wealth Advisory. They directed me to Telegram group @PrimeReturnsVIP and requested ₹90,000 in bank account 456789XXXX5528. My investment vanished within two days.

title: Drone Marketplace Fraud
description: An online seller advertised a camera drone at half the market price. They instructed me to send ₹27,000 to rajeshstore@okaxis. The seller claimed to belong to Used Deal Shop and contacted me from +91 9988776655.

title: Matrimonial Profile Fraud
description: I met someone through a matrimonial website who claimed to be working abroad. After several conversations they requested financial assistance for an emergency. I transferred ₹55,000 before realizing the profile was fake.

title: Fake Electricity Bill Scam
description: I received an SMS stating that my electricity connection would be disconnected unless I paid an overdue bill immediately. The message contained a fraudulent payment link. After making the payment, I discovered the electricity department had not sent the message.

title: PAN Verification Fraud in SBI
description: Someone called me from +91 9876543210 claiming my PAN was not linked with my State Bank of India account. They asked me to pay ₹12,000 through securekyc@ibl to avoid account suspension. After payment, the number was switched off.

title: Job Offer Registration Scam
description: A company offered me a work-from-home position but requested a registration fee of ₹4,500 before issuing the appointment letter. After payment, all contact details became inactive.

title: Furniture Advance Payment Scam
description: I tried buying a sofa set from Used Deal Shop through an online marketplace. I transferred ₹11,000 to rajeshstore@okaxis after speaking with the seller on +91 9988776655. The seller blocked my number after payment.

title: Gold Investment Scheme Scam
description: Prime Wealth Advisory advertised a gold investment plan with guaranteed monthly returns. I communicated with their representative on +91 9765432109 and Telegram @PrimeReturnsVIP before transferring ₹65,000 to account 456789XXXX5528. The promised returns never arrived.

title: Courier GST Collection Fraud
description: I received an email claiming my imported parcel required GST payment before release. The email directed me to https://trackparcel-support.in and customer support number +91 9123456780. I transferred ₹7,600 to customfees@paytm but never received any shipment.

title: Fake Delivery Charge Fraud
description: A caller from +91 9123456780 claimed to be from Express Logistics India and informed me that my parcel required an additional delivery charge. I transferred ₹2,500 to customfees@paytm after checking the tracking details on https://trackparcel-support.in. My parcel never arrived.

title: Debit Card Block Scam
description: A fraudster pretending to be from State Bank of India informed me that my debit card had been blocked due to incomplete KYC. I was instructed to transfer ₹9,500 to securekyc@ibl using phone number +91 9876543210. After payment, there was no response.

title: Cryptocurrency Investment Fraud
description: A representative of Prime Wealth Advisory contacted me from +91 9765432109 and invited me to join Telegram channel @PrimeReturnsVIP. They convinced me to invest ₹1,20,000 in cryptocurrency through bank account 456789XXXX5528. After the transfer, they stopped responding.

title: CKYC Renewal Fraud
description: I received a call from +91 9876543210 saying my CKYC registration had expired. The caller claimed to be an SBI officer and directed me to pay ₹18,000 to securekyc@ibl to complete the renewal. Later I realized it was a scam.

title: QR Code Payment Scam
description: A stranger claimed they needed to send me money and asked me to scan a QR code. After scanning it, ₹9,500 was deducted from my bank account instead of being credited.

title: Fake RBI and SBI KYC Update
description: A caller claiming to represent RBI and State Bank of India contacted me from +91 9876543210. I was told to complete KYC verification by sending ₹24,000 to securekyc@ibl. They promised the money would be refunded after verification but I lost the amount.

title: Guaranteed Stock Returns Fraud
description: I was promised 30 percent monthly returns by Prime Wealth Advisory through Telegram @PrimeReturnsVIP. The representative using phone number +91 9765432109 instructed me to deposit ₹1,50,000 into bank account 456789XXXX5528. After payment, all communication channels disappeared.

title: Internet Banking Activation Fraud
description: I was contacted by a fake SBI customer support executive from +91 9876543210 who claimed my internet banking could only be activated after paying ₹5,000 to securekyc@ibl. After transferring the money, the caller stopped responding.

title: Parcel Insurance Payment Scam
description: I was told that my shipment required mandatory insurance before delivery. The representative from Express Logistics India instructed me to pay ₹5,400 through customfees@paytm. The communication came from +91 9123456780 and the tracking page was https://trackparcel-support.in. The shipment was fake.

title: Laptop Marketplace Fraud
description: A marketplace listing offered a gaming laptop at a discounted price. The seller requested ₹29,000 as an advance payment to rajeshstore@okaxis. The seller identified themselves as Used Deal Shop and provided support@useddealshop.com for communication. After payment the listing was removed.

title: SBI Account Freeze Threat
description: A fraudster using phone number +91 9876543210 claimed my SBI account would be permanently frozen due to expired KYC. I paid ₹31,000 through UPI ID securekyc@ibl hoping to restore my account. The fraudster vanished after receiving the payment.

title: iPhone Marketplace Advance Payment Scam
description: I found an advertisement for a second-hand iPhone on an online marketplace. The seller from Used Deal Shop asked me to transfer ₹18,000 as an advance to UPI ID rajeshstore@okaxis. The seller communicated through phone number +91 9988776655 and email support@useddealshop.com. After the payment, the advertisement disappeared and the seller stopped responding.

title: Address Verification Courier Scam
description: A fraudster pretending to work for Express Logistics India called me from +91 9123456780 and claimed my parcel could not be delivered due to an address mismatch. I was asked to pay ₹1,800 using customfees@paytm after verifying the parcel on https://trackparcel-support.in. The parcel did not exist.

title: AI Trading Platform Scam
description: I was promised daily profits through an AI-powered trading platform operated by Prime Wealth Advisory. The fraudster communicated using +91 9765432109 and Telegram @PrimeReturnsVIP. I deposited ₹58,000 into bank account 456789XXXX5528 and never recovered the money.

title: International Parcel Customs Fee Scam
description: I received an SMS stating that my international parcel was being held by Express Logistics India. The message directed me to visit https://trackparcel-support.in and contact customer support at +91 9123456780. I was instructed to pay ₹8,900 to UPI ID customfees@paytm for customs clearance. After making the payment, the tracking website stopped working.

title: Mobile Number Verification Scam
description: The caller from +91 9876543210 informed me that my registered mobile number required urgent verification with State Bank of India. Believing the caller, I transferred ₹8,000 to securekyc@ibl. The phone became unreachable afterwards.

title: Courier Holding Charge Scam
description: I received a call stating that my parcel was being held at a warehouse. The caller asked me to pay ₹3,200 to customfees@paytm. The caller identified themselves as Express Logistics India using phone number +91 9123456780 and directed me to https://trackparcel-support.in. The parcel never existed.

title: Intraday Trading Fraud
description: I received stock trading tips from Prime Wealth Advisory after joining Telegram @PrimeReturnsVIP. The advisor instructed me to deposit ₹42,000 into bank account 456789XXXX5528. The contact number used throughout the communication was +91 9765432109. The group disappeared after collecting payments.

title: Fake Customer Care Refund Scam
description: I searched online for customer care of an e-commerce website and called the first number that appeared. The representative convinced me to install a remote access application to process a refund. Money was transferred from my account without authorization.

title: Fake SBI Account Verification Fee
description: A caller claiming to be an officer from State Bank of India contacted me from +91 9876543210 and requested ₹27,000 for mandatory account verification through securekyc@ibl. After transferring the money, I was unable to contact the person again.

title: International Shipment Release Fraud
description: Someone pretending to be from Express Logistics India claimed my overseas shipment required immediate release charges. I transferred ₹9,800 to customfees@paytm after confirming the fake tracking details at https://trackparcel-support.in. The fraudster became unreachable afterwards.

title: Fraudulent Charity Donation Request
description: I received a social media message requesting donations for flood relief. The campaign appeared genuine, but after donating ₹15,000 I learned the charity organization did not exist.

title: Fake MacBook Seller Fraud
description: A person claiming to represent Used Deal Shop offered a MacBook at a discounted price. They requested an advance payment of ₹32,000 to rajeshstore@okaxis. The contact number was +91 9988776655 and further communication happened through support@useddealshop.com. After payment I never received the product.

title: Redelivery Fee Scam
description: Express Logistics India informed me that my delivery had failed and required a redelivery fee of ₹950. I made the payment to customfees@paytm after speaking with +91 9123456780 and checking the parcel status on https://trackparcel-support.in. It turned out to be fraudulent.

title: Aadhaar Linking Scam
description: A person claiming to work for State Bank of India called from +91 9876543210 and informed me that my Aadhaar was not linked with my account. I was instructed to transfer ₹15,500 to securekyc@ibl as a verification charge. The fraudster disappeared after receiving the money.

title: Camera Purchase Scam
description: I contacted Used Deal Shop regarding a DSLR camera. They requested ₹25,500 through UPI ID rajeshstore@okaxis. The communication happened using support@useddealshop.com and phone number +91 9988776655. After receiving payment they disappeared.

title: PlayStation 5 Marketplace Scam
description: I attempted to purchase a PlayStation 5 through an online marketplace. The seller instructed me to transfer ₹20,000 to rajeshstore@okaxis before dispatch. The seller claimed to work for Used Deal Shop and used phone number +91 9988776655. The product was never shipped.
